sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'M' and n['pixels'] == 4:
        center = (sum(p[0] for p in n['boundary'])/len(n['boundary']),
                  sum(p[1] for p in n['boundary'])/len(n['boundary']))
        if center[0] > 5:
            print(f"M block: center~({center[0]:.0f},{center[1]:.0f})")

# Previous positions: (32, 20), (40, 36), (56, 46), (56, 16)
# I clicked (22, 33) - the nearest block to (22, 33) would be (32, 20)
# Distance from (32, 20) to (22, 33) is about sqrt(100 + 169) = 16.4
# Distance from (40, 36) to (22, 33) is about sqrt(324 + 9) = 18.2
# So (32, 20) should be the nearest
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The blocks still didn't move! But the board changed. Let me check what actually changed. Maybe the white bar at the bottom is changing, not the blocks.
Wait, let me re-examine. When I clicked (35, 19) and (32, 20), the block at (36, 18) moved. But now clicking (22, 33) doesn't move anything. What's different?
Oh wait - maybe the blocks only move when clicked on a position that's within a certain distance? Or maybe the blocks only move one step at a time, and the block at (32, 20) is too far from (22, 33)?
Let me check: when the block was at (36, 18) and I clicked (35, 19), the distance was about sqrt(1 + 1) = 1.4. Then when it was at (34, 18) and I clicked (32, 20), the distance was about sqrt(4 + 4) = 2.8.
Now the block is at (32, 20) and I clicked (22, 33), which is distance sqrt(100 + 169) = 16.4. That's much farther!
Maybe blocks only move when the click is within a certain range? Let me try clicking closer to the block.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Maybe blocks only move when clicked within a certain range.
# Let me try clicking closer to the block at (32, 20).
# Click at (30, 22) - 2 rows up, 2 cols right from the block center
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 30, 'col': 22}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
